---
title: "Setup Guide"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

The PRISM ecosystem consists of a backend server built with FastAPI and a web frontend built with Flutter. Both rely on Firebase for authentication and database storage. This guide provides instructions on how to set up the PRISM system locally for development or deployment.

## Which setup should I use?

For most users and researchers, we recommend using the **Unified Setup via Docker**. It is the quickest and most reliable way to run the entire PRISM ecosystem (both frontend and backend) without manually configuring language runtimes.

You should only use the **Manual Setup** if:
* You are a developer planning to modify the backend code or the Flutter frontend web app code directly.
* You need to debug a specific service locally outside of a container environment.

---

## Unified Setup via Docker

The easiest way to install and run both the PRISM Backend and Frontend is via the [`prism-setup` repository](https://github.com/Australian-Future-Hearing-Initiative/prism-setup), which uses Docker Compose.

{{< include _setup_readme.qmd >}}

---

## Manual Setup

If you prefer to run and develop the services independently without Docker, follow the manual instructions below.

### System Architecture Overview

The PRISM platform is split into two components:
1. **Backend Server (FastAPI)**: A Python-based REST API that communicates with Firebase Auth, Firestore, and handles data storage/exports.
2. **Research Portal Frontend (Flutter)**: A web-based user interface used by researchers to manage experiments and view participant session data.

Both components connect to the same **Firebase** project, which must be created before starting the setup.

### Manual Backend Setup

Follow these instructions to run the FastAPI backend independently.

{{< include _flutter-backend_readme.qmd >}}

### Manual Frontend Setup

Follow these instructions to run the Flutter web portal independently.

{{< include _flutter-web-frontend_readme.qmd >}}

---

## Setup Checklist

Before deploying or launching PRISM, verify that you have completed all setup steps:

- [ ] Created a Firebase project with paid (Blaze) usage enabled.
- [ ] Registered a Web App within your Firebase project.
- [ ] Generated and saved the Firebase Service Account key as `serviceAccountKey.json`.
- [ ] Added the Firebase Web API Key to your configuration/environment variables.
- [ ] Set up SMTP credentials (Mailgun or Gmail App Password) for participant invite emails.
- [ ] Created a secure random string for `JWT_SECRET`.
- [ ] Configured matching API URLs between the frontend and backend (for manual setups).
